question: David is now 4 years more than twice the age of Henry. If Henry will be 18 in 3 years, how old was David 2 years ago?

solution.strategy: The ages are given in three different time frames. Do not confuse them!
steps: Let Henry's age now = x. David's age now = 2x + 4.
Henry's age in 3 years = x + 3 = 18. Thus, Henry's age now = 15.
David's age now = 2(15) + 4 = 34.
David's age 2 years ago = 34 - 2 = 32.

question: Maria is now 3 years more than twice the age of Nina. If Nina will be 20 in 5 years, how old was Maria 3 years ago?

solution.strategy: The ages are given in three different time frames. Do not confuse them!
steps: Let Nina's age now = x. Maria's age now = 2x + 3.
Nina's age in 5 years = x + 5 = 20. Thus, Nina's age now = 15.
Maria's age now = 2(15) + 3 = 33.
Maria's age 3 years ago = 33 - 3 = 30.

question: Tom is now 5 years more than twice the age of Sam. If Sam will be 17 in 3 years, how old was Tom 1 year ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Sam's age now = x. Tom's age now = 2x + 5.
Sam's age in 3 years = x + 3 = 17, so x = 14.
Tom's age now = 2(14) + 5 = 33.
Tom's age 1 year ago = 33 - 1 = 32.

question: Emma is now 2 years more than twice the age of Jake. If Jake will be 16 in 4 years, how old was Emma 2 years ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Jake's age now = x. Emma's age now = 2x + 2.
Jake's age in 4 years = x + 4 = 16, so x = 12.
Emma's age now = 2(12) + 2 = 26.
Emma's age 2 years ago = 26 - 2 = 24.

question: Carlos is now 6 years more than twice the age of Lily. If Lily will be 14 in 2 years, how old was Carlos 4 years ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Lily's age now = x. Carlos's age now = 2x + 6.
Lily's age in 2 years = x + 2 = 14, so x = 12.
Carlos's age now = 2(12) + 6 = 30.
Carlos's age 4 years ago = 30 - 4 = 26.

question: Rachel is now 4 years more than twice the age of Ben. If Ben will be 21 in 6 years, how old was Rachel 1 year ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Ben's age now = x. Rachel's age now = 2x + 4.
Ben's age in 6 years = x + 6 = 21, so x = 15.
Rachel's age now = 2(15) + 4 = 34.
Rachel's age 1 year ago = 34 - 1 = 33.

question: Paul is now 3 years more than twice the age of Anna. If Anna will be 19 in 5 years, how old was Paul 3 years ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Anna's age now = x. Paul's age now = 2x + 3.
Anna's age in 5 years = x + 5 = 19, so x = 14.
Paul's age now = 2(14) + 3 = 31.
Paul's age 3 years ago = 31 - 3 = 28.

question: Kate is now 5 years more than twice the age of Leo. If Leo will be 12 in 2 years, how old was Kate 5 years ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Leo's age now = x. Kate's age now = 2x + 5.
Leo's age in 2 years = x + 2 = 12, so x = 10.
Kate's age now = 2(10) + 5 = 25.
Kate's age 5 years ago = 25 - 5 = 20.

question: James is now 4 years more than twice the age of Sofia. If Sofia will be 22 in 4 years, how old was James 2 years ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Sofia's age now = x. James's age now = 2x + 4.
Sofia's age in 4 years = x + 4 = 22, so x = 18.
James's age now = 2(18) + 4 = 40.
James's age 2 years ago = 40 - 2 = 38.

question: Olivia is now 2 years more than twice the age of Ethan. If Ethan will be 15 in 3 years, how old was Olivia 4 years ago?

solution.strategy: Define variables and set up equations from the time frames.
steps: Let Ethan's age now = x. Olivia's age now = 2x + 2.
Ethan's age in 3 years = x + 3 = 15, so x = 12.
Olivia's age now = 2(12) + 2 = 26.
Olivia's age 4 years ago = 26 - 4 = 22.
